author: luca_zanolini
content: Not sure if given the attack (and assuming a builder has that power, which seems unlikely), this would solve it tho? The idea of the attack is that the builder splits the network such that the validators that get the right payload will get it 'too late' so that the proposer does not have time to retrieve it before proposing, and it will build on EMPTY. Would this change solve this?

author: luca_zanolini
content: I'm writing the proofs given the assumption I wrote above (which should reflect what Potuz mentioned)

author: terencechain
content:
Because the attesters will forward it to the proposer and the proposer would actually accept it. Vs. current gossip rule says you can only accept the first payload you have received.
But ya if this goes after second 0 of new slot then it is "too late"

author: luca_zanolini
content:
Yes exactly that is my understanding of the attack. But probably this addition is worth adding nevertheless? 

Do you know how is ExecutionPayloadEnvelopesByRoot used? I thought that this could be used to retrieve more  payloads in case the one you received was invalid

author: terencechain
content:
its for short range recovery.. examples...
- attestation pointing there's a full block, but i have not seen its payload..
- block building on top of full, but i cant sync the block because i dont have the payload
i dont think we have coded behavior that we assume a peer is malicious in a way and try to recover from an invalid payload. It's an interesting thought tho, and doable

author: luca_zanolini
content:
<@363800010518822915> Beacon-block gossip already IGNOREs + "MAY queue" a block whose parent execution payload hasn't been seen/retrieved.

But `on_block` asserts the stronger `is_payload_verified(block.parent_root)` (parent in store.payloads), with no queue note.

Can a block pass gossip (parent seen) yet hit the `on_block` assert because the parent isn't verified yet, and get dropped — then stay dropped even after the parent later lands in store.payloads, since nothing re-runs `on_block`? That would cost an honest block its votes and open a reorg. Or does the gossip queue guarantee `on_block` only runs once the parent is verified?

author: terencechain
content: @terence Beacon-block gossip already

author: terencechain
content: <@1386598056320831509> For auth transport (builder api),  is it settled? Will SignedRequestAuthV1 ride in the request body (JSON/SSZ with Eth-Consensus-Version), or move to JasonVranek's X-Eth-Request-Auth-Signature header?

author: bharath.vedartham
content: We agreed to put it in the request body instead of as a signature in the header. It is better for the builder to receive the full object so that they know what message to verify the signature with

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
I am considering merging https://github.com/ethereum/builder-specs/pull/138 on Thursday morning IST, if devs have any feedback on the PR please do provide by then. 
We can always iterate on it

author: terencechain
content:
perfect, is the following correct?
- pubkey vs index in get bid. We use pub key
- request timeout, not configurable
- batch auth. Not supported yet. Just one auth per builder

author: bharath.vedartham
content:
1. we use pubkey yes
2. I didn't understand this? there is an optional header to pass the timeout to?
3. one auth per builder yes

author: bharath.vedartham
content: do you mean whether a client can configure their getBid timeout?

author: terencechain
content: ya.. and we dont support it right? i saw there's some back and forth about it

author: bharath.vedartham
content: we haven't explicitly decided to make it configurable or not. I think builders would like it, if it is configurable

author: bharath.vedartham
content: i think <@755590043632140352> was against doing it and that we set it to a default of 1s or so like we have today. I dont think the timeouts are configurable today

author: terencechain
content: i wont do it for now and just do a default. ill have the timeout set at 500ms which should be mroe than enough

author: bharath.vedartham
content: yeah we can revisit this later. dont think its a big change for clients in the worse case we flip. But I dont think we should do it and risk making timing games easier in the protocol

author: bharath.vedartham
content: Would something like https://github.com/ethereum/builder-specs/pull/144 be useful for clients. In this PR, I tried to define how to score bids across p2p bids, builder-api bids and local bid considering the min-bid, local block boost, max_execution_payment etc. I wonder if clients see any value in having something like this

author: bharath.vedartham
content: if it is valuable, I can clean and update this PR more

author: potuz
content: I say we need to set it to less. I do not mind if we make it configurable TBH, I'm pretty sure some CL client will do it and there goes the argument of client diversity. The right way of doing this is to actually make the attestation deadline as short as possible. cc <@792404665068158998> <@968526635735326741>, if we manage to move to 2 seconds instead of 3 I think we should do it... and stop this timing game shenanigans right before they start happening.
